Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows significantly elevated creatinine in both serum and blood, indicating impaired renal function, and marked proteinuria and microalbuminuria, suggesting possible nephropathy. Urine is abnormal in appearance and color, with low specific gravity. HbA1c is low, which may reflect recent hypoglycemia or anemia. LDL cholesterol is mildly elevated. Findings are consistent with chronic kidney disease, likely with nephrotic-range proteinuria; further nephrology evaluation is recommended.
